Does anyone know on why this happens or how to resolve this?
 
Thanks & Regards.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: SAP and Databricks open a bold new era of data and AI - BDC2767
entry.content:
At this year’s SAP Sapphire, I’m excited to share groundbreaking innovations that are changing the way organizations leverage data. Join me to explore how SAP and Databricks are coming together to transform enterprise data strategies and accelerate the path to AI.

📅Session Details:

Title:
Unleashing the Value of Business Data with SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks

When:

  • Tuesday, May 20 – 2:00 PM to 2:20 PM
  • Wednesday, May 21 – 1:30 PM to 1:50 PM

Where:
SAP Sapphire – Session Link

What You’ll Learn:

SAP and Databricks announced a landmark partnership back in February that will redefine how applications and data platforms work together. From curated SAP data products to zero-copy integration, this session will show how SAP Databricks within the SAP Business Data Cloud solution empowers data professionals to:

  • Accelerate the development and deployment of AI models and data applications
  • Unlock trusted, business-context-rich data without duplication
  • Seamlessly connect SAP data to the Databricks Lakehouse Platform
  • Deliver real-time, intelligent insights that drive value across the enterprise

Whether you're responsible for data strategy, analytics, or innovation, this session will equip you with actionable insights to elevate your organization’s data capabilities.

Co-Speaker: I am excited to be joined by former colleague and longtime friend Brian Suk, a Senior Solutions Architect at Databricks.

About Me: Kara Reed
I’m a Senior Director of Product Marketing at SAP, where I focus on helping organizations adopt and maximize the value of SAP Business Data Cloud and other cloud-based data solutions. Over the past 14 years at SAP, I’ve held a variety of roles in the Data & Analytics organization, most recently on the Retail Solution Management team. I work closely with customers and industry leaders to guide our go-to-market strategies and support the data transformation journey.

The fun doesn't stop there! Check out these other sessions to learn more about this game changing partnership and the value SAP Databricks within SAP Business Data Cloud brings!

An architect’s guide: SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Databricks solutions - BDC2633

  • Tuesday, May 20 1:00-1:30

SAP Databricks: Simplify analytics and AI for your SAP software data - PAR1122 – customer success story

  • Tuesday, May 20 1:30-1:50

How Baker Hughes modernizes its applications with SAP Business Data Cloud - BDC3260 – customer success story

  • Tuesday, May 20 2:30-2:50

Unlock the potential of AI with the SAP Databricks solution - PAR1123 – customer success story

  • Wednesday, May 21 11:00-11:20

Can't make it to SAP Sapphire? No problem! Follow along the tags and topic pages below to stay up to date on the latest SAP data and analytics news!

Tags: Data and Analytics SAP Datasphere SAP Business Data Cloud

Topic pages:  Data and Analytics  SAP Business Data Cloud  SAP Datasphere

Now I would like to hear from you:

  • Which use cases in your business could be accelerated or simplified by leveraging the zero-copy approach of SAP Databricks?

entry.title: Combining Freight order according to carrier for Printout
entry.content:
Hi,

We are doing greenfield implementation of S4 HANA 2023. We do not have advance TM but basic embedded TM.

The requirement is to print a note to carrier which contains all freight orders on the day with different ship to party. They do it for each carrier. So, a single printout containing all freight orders with same carrier but different ship to. The print contains basic details of all freight orders like number, ship to, quantity etc

I checked but could not find any standard way to do that. Read about the consignment order but it is I believe in advance TM. Is there a standard way to achieve this.

If not, I was thinking about custom methods. Freight order lists are there in Freight order worklist app, pick according to the carrier and collate it in a custom pdf created for the purpose. 

 Is there any other app which allows you to take collective print of FO.

entry.title: Re: HOW TO GET NOTIFICATION IN MY INBOX FIORI APP WHEN PA...
entry.content:
Got it! To ensure the invoice parking notification appears in the SAP Fiori My Inbox app , you'll need to integrate SAP Workflow with the Fiori My Inbox framework . Here’s how you can achieve that:

Steps to Display Notifications in My Inbox:
1. Verify the Workflow Task Configuration
- Use Transaction SWDD to check if a workflow task is triggered when an invoice is parked.
- Ensure the task is configured correctly with an appropriate recipient role .

2. Map the Workflow Task to Fiori My Inbox
- Open Transaction SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1 and check if your workflow task ID is mapped to push notifications.
- If it isn't, create a new mapping and associate it with My Inbox .

3. Activate the Required OData Services
- Ensure SAP Gateway services for My Inbox are active using Transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE .
- Activate the relevant service (`/IWPGW/TASKPROCESSING`) for workflow integration.

4. Ensure the Task is "User Decision" Compatible
- The workflow task needs to be compatible with User Decision processing to appear in My Inbox.
- Use Transaction SWDD to validate the task type and make modifications if necessary.

5. Configure Notifications in SSB2
- Go to Transaction SSB2 and confirm that My Inbox notifications are enabled .
- Assign the necessary catalog roles (`SAP_BR_AUDITOR` or custom roles) to ensure visibility.

6. Testing & Troubleshooting
- Trigger an invoice parking scenario manually.
- Verify that the notification appears in SBWP and also in My Inbox .
- If it still doesn't work, check Transaction SWF_PUSH_NOTIF1 for any missing configurations.

Alternative Approach: Custom Enhancement
If standard configuration doesn’t push notifications to My Inbox, you can implement an ABAP enhancement using BAdi WORKFLOW_TASKS_INBOX or extend the SAPUI5 My Inbox app to fetch workflow notifications explicitly.

entry.title: Allocate data from parent to child node
entry.content:
Hello, SAC community:

I have a dimension product with 2 hierarchies, one with multiple levels and the other one with the parents as root members. It looks like this:

 

In H1, 010105 has 0101 as parent, and 0101 has 01 as parent. In H2, only 01 appears as <root>. I'd like to introduce data at parent level using H2 and then be able to allocate it to its children equally or with weights depending in another year, for example. Is there any way to do this in a Data Action?

I've also thought about creating some dummy leaf members and showing these when the user inputs the data, but I don't know how to allocate the data if the data is in a leaf member. Doing a loop seems useless since it would need a parameter for each member that tells the number of siblings it has, and that just feels like bad desing.

 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Express document "Update was terminated" for BAPI BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2
entry.content:
Hello Karthik,

I tried with Update function module which also gives same errors but at different location.

I have added Update FM code below.

 

CALL FUNCTION 'ZFM_UPDATE_PO_CHANGE' IN UPDATE TASK
  EXPORTING
    PURCHASEORDER       = T_EKET-EBELN
 TABLES
*   RETURN              = RETURN[]
*   POITEM              = P_T_ITEM[]
*   POITEMX             = P_T_ITEMX[]
   POSCHEDULE          = T_POSCHEDULE
   POSCHEDULEX         = T_POSCHEDULEX .
*   AFS_POITEM          = P_T_POITEM[]
*   AFS_POITEMX         = P_T_POITEMX[] .

FUNCTION ZFM_UPDATE_PO_CHANGE.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Update Function Module:
*"
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(PURCHASEORDER) LIKE  BAPIMEPOHEADER-PO_NUMBER
*"  TABLES
*"      RETURN STRUCTURE  BAPIRET2 OPTIONAL
*"      POITEM STRUCTURE  BAPIMEPOITEM OPTIONAL
*"      POITEMX STRUCTURE  BAPIMEPOITEMX OPTIONAL
*"      POSCHEDULE STRUCTURE  BAPIMEPOSCHEDULE OPTIONAL
*"      POSCHEDULEX STRUCTURE  BAPIMEPOSCHEDULX OPTIONAL
*"      AFS_POITEM STRUCTURE  /AFS/BAPIMEPOITEM OPTIONAL
*"      AFS_POITEMX STRUCTURE  /AFS/BAPIMEPOITEMX OPTIONAL
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

DATA: LWA_ZBAPIRET2 TYPE ZBAPIRET2 .
CLEAR:LWA_ZBAPIRET2 .

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE'
    EXPORTING
      PURCHASEORDER = PURCHASEORDER "P_T_EKET_EBELN
    TABLES
      RETURN        = RETURN
      POITEM        = POITEM[]        "P_T_ITEM[]
      POITEMX       = POITEMX[]       "P_T_ITEMX[]
      POSCHEDULE    = POSCHEDULE[]    "P_T_POSCHED[]
      POSCHEDULEX   = POSCHEDULEX[]   "P_T_POSCHEDX[]
      AFS_POITEM    = AFS_POITEM[]    "P_T_POITEM[]
      AFS_POITEMX   = AFS_POITEMX[].  "P_T_POITEMX[].

LOOP AT RETURN WHERE TYPE EQ 'E' OR TYPE EQ 'A' OR TYPE EQ 'S'.
  LWA_ZBAPIRET2-TYPE           = RETURN-TYPE     .
  LWA_ZBAPIRET2-MESSAGE        = RETURN-MESSAGE     .
MODIFY ZBAPIRET2 FROM LWA_ZBAPIRET2.
CLEAR LWA_ZBAPIRET2  .
ENDLOOP.

ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: 📅 Day 6/30 – Routings in SAP PP: Mapping the Path to Production
entry.content:
Once your materials are in place and Work Centers are defined, it’s time to organize the production steps — and that’s where Routings come in!

In SAP PP, Routings describe the sequence of operations required to manufacture a product. It’s the production navigation map that tells SAP how and where to make things.

🔍What Does a Routing Contain?

Each routing includes:

🔸Operation steps (e.g., cut, drill, assemble)

🔸Assigned Work Centers

🔸Standard values (e.g., setup time, processing time)

🔸Control keys (influences whether confirmation, printing, or costing is required)

🔸Inspection characteristics (optional integration with QM)

🧩 How Are Routings Used in SAP PP?

✅Basis for Production Order creation

✅Drives Scheduling and Capacity Planning

✅Supports Cost Estimation

✅Enables Process Optimization

 

Real-Life Example:

If you’re manufacturing a steel chair:

1️⃣Cut steel sheets

2️⃣Bend into shape

3️⃣Weld joints

4️⃣Paint

5️⃣Final assembly

Each of these is an operation in the Routing, with time, tools, and resources assigned.

✅Pro Tip:

Use Reference Operation Sets and Routing Groups to reduce duplication and standardize processes across similar materials. This is key in repetitive or discrete manufacturing!

💬Have you built or optimized Routings before? What’s your experience with managing variant-specific Routings or frequent process changes?

entry.title: Streamlining Communication: Initiating a Whatsapp Chat Right from SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Introduction

Effective and Timely communications are the key to Success.With Instant and effective communications, great milestones can be achieved.By integrating Social Account Instant Messaging functionality directly within Employee Central, companies can streamline communication, boost productivity, and significantly enhance the employee experience.Enabling Small and important features like this is going to make a lot of change in the way SuccessFactors is used and also ensures Users and HR's make the most out of the System.

No more Copy Pasting URL's / Numbers from One Tab/Application to the Other.With Instant Messaging HRIS Element , Initiating Instant chats are just a Click Away!

Configurations :

Step 1 : Enable the iminfo HRIS Element from the Manage Business Configuration

Step 2 : Create a Picklist with the Social Account names you require and assign it to the field Domain

 

Step 3 : Create a onchange rule with Base Object as Social Accounts Information Model (Based on the Label , The Base Object Name may Vary) and assign it to the domain field

 

Step 4 : Now grant the necessary permissions under Manage Permission Roles to access the Social Accounts Information

Step 5 : Now go to the People Profile of the Employee and maintain the Details under Contact Information Social Accounts Information Block

Once you select the domain as Whatsapp , The Rule will be triggered and the URL will be set as https://wa.me (for whatsapp) and the number is to be maintained in the Username field without the + symbol and country code is mandatory. For example if the number belongs to India , it should be filled as 91123456789 where the 91 denotes the country code.

Step 6 : Click on the value maintained against the Whatsapp field and it will redirect to the whatsapp page where chat can be initiated as below.By Default , the link will be opened in the same page , you can right click and open the link in a new tab if you want it to be opened in a new page.

Click on continue to chat and you can use Whatsapp web if the Whatsapp Desktop Application is not installed.

This is not limited to Whatsapp and can be extended to other social media accounts like FaceBook and so on.

Conclusion :

The future of HR communication is here—and it’s fast, efficient, and user-friendly. By integrating Instant Messaging functionality directly into SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, you’re not only improving communication within your organization but also enhancing the overall employee experience.

Feel Free to drop your thought and comments

entry.title: Averaging in SAP Payroll using AVERA
entry.content:
Averaging in SAP Payroll

Introduction

SAP Payroll consultants are sometimes faced with a requirement where the rate of payment for a certain wage type needs to be an average rate of a sum of some other rates over a certain number of previous periods.

Real-life examples in Australia include:

  1. “Where, prior to the taking of leave, an employee was paid a uniform allowance other than at the weekly rate, the rate to be paid during absence on leave will be the average of the allowance paid during the 4 weeks immediately preceding the taking of leave.” From https://awards.fairwork.gov.au/MA000027.html
  2. “Part-time Employee – the Employee’s contracted hours, save that where the part-time Employee’s ordinary hours fluctuate because the Employee works additional ordinary shifts (but excluding a permanent variation) an average of the Employee’s ordinary hours over: A. the preceding 12 months; or B. the Employee’s period of service where the Employee has less than 12 months service; will apply where this is more favourable to the Employee” from https://vahpa.asn.au/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/AHP-Vic-Public-Sector-Single-Interest-Employers-EA-2021-26.pdf

Analysis

In the interest of fully implementing such complex legislative requirements, we are faced with the following options:

  1. Manually calculate the average outside the system and input it in payroll, thus simplifying the calculation
  2. Create a custom function/operation to calculate the average of a set of wage types, which gives more flexibility in ABAP but increases the complexity
  3. Leverage standard SAP functionality to average wage types and show the end user a full log of what’s happening under the hood

Option 3 is the cleanest way to do it but there are very few examples. Options 1 and 2 are mostly favoured and in the interest of time, projects go live before anyone investigates further. SAP Payroll has the standard AVERA function which does this heavy work very cleanly and you can read Technical Process of Average Processing (New) as a reference guide when implementing it.

Payroll -> Payroll: Australia -> Time and Payment/Deduction Wage Type Valuation -> Averages (New) has all the configuration nodes to enable this, although these tables are available in almost every SAP Payroll country’s configuration node.

 

Figure 0 SPRO Node for Averages (New)

Step-by-Step T51AV* Configuration

Let’s take an example of averaging step-by-step. Suppose the requirement is for payroll to calculate the average of overtime/penalty amounts and hours over the past 6 weeks.

1.     V_T51AV_2W Primary wage type to Average Bases

Assign the “to-be-averaged” or "seed" wage type to an "average base" /2XX wage type in V_T51AV_2W.

Figure 1 Seed Wage type to Average Base

V_T51AV_2P has options to prorate based on TASOLL, TKSOLL or TSSOLL but we won’t be needing that here.

The next is a critical sequence of 8 steps culminating in “Calculation Rules for Averages”.

Figure 2 SPRO Subactivities

2.     V_T51AV_R Relevance Rules

Create a custom relevance rule for your case in V_T51AV_R. All payroll periods are relevant. There are options to exclude relevance of some periods based on rehiring, partial overlaps, etc.

Figure 2.1 Relevance Rules

3.     V_T51AV_ROC Exclude Offcycle Reasons

If you want to exclude from averaging for example, a set of penalties paid as part of a one-off offcycle bonus payment, then this can be configured in V_T51AV_ROC.

Figure 3 Exclude Offcycle Reasons

 

4.     V_T51AV_C Adjustment Rules

To cater for scenarios where payscale increases can increase the value of the underlying “to-be-averaged” wage type, use V_T51AV_C.

 

5.     V_T51AV_B Cumulation Rules

Configure a new entry in V_T51AV_B cumulating the number and the amount fields and specifying a factoring specification F to ensure that penalties are prorated as per calendar days if only part of a payroll period is to be averaged.

Figure 4 Cumulation Rules

6.     PCR Q018 or a custom-copy of it

AVERA allows call of a custom PCR (copied from standard Aus PCR Q018) to finally divide the total by the number of average periods

 

Figure 5 Standard Calculation PCR

 

For this requirement, we create a custom PCR to store the number of average periods taken into account into a variable.

Figure 6 Custom Copy of PCR Q018

 

7.     V_T51AV_E Linkage of PCR

We link this custom PCR to a newly configured Final Processing Rule in V_T51AV_E.

 

Figure 7 Linkage of PCR

8.     V_T51AV_W Comparison Rules

If there is a requirement to compare this averaged value to another wage type within that period, then this can be configured within V_T51AV_W. This is useful in cases where the legislation requires the higher of two rates to be paid, or if the customer simply wants to intervene and provide a higher rate.

9.     V_T51AV_A Calculation Rule for Averages

We tie it all together in V_T51AV_A and provide 6 calendar weeks as the time period to average (3 fortnightly payroll periods).

Figure 8 Calculation Rule

 

The custom rule specified in V_T51AV_E always places the average in the rate field of a resultant /1XX wage type linked with the V_T51AV_A rule in table V_T51AV_P.

 

AVERA does not get activated if we attempt to place a custom wage type instead of a /1XX wage type, so this may be a standard limitation.

 

Hence, in this example, a /199 wage type needs to be created to hold the final average rate.

10.    V_T51AV_P Average Bases to Output Wage Type

 

As you can see below, the resultant wage type and the average rule have now been linked.

 

Figure 9 Average Base to Output WT

 

 

 

Payroll Testing

We are now ready to run payroll and look at the result.

 

Figure 10 Payroll Periods

Drilling down into AVERA function in the payroll log, we get the following picture.

 Figure 11 Averaging Time Slices

 

Remember, we specified in V_T51AV_A that we are using weeks as the time unit. To determine what is the last day of a week, we also incorporate a change in Feature LDAYW, that makes Wednesday the last day of a week, which is in line with the payroll period last date.

Figure 12 Feature LDAYW

 

 

Understanding the calculation

If you look at the underlying Infotype 2010 records that actually averaged out, you can see that they have the same number of hours. In reality, these wage types may originate from Infotype 2002 (Time evaluation in ECP / On-premise SAP Payroll) or from Infotype 14/15 or even be a basic pay allowance.

 

Our scenario here is an EC Payroll example which gets these entries into Infotype 2010 from EC Timesheet / SF Time Tracking / External Time system

 

Figure 13 Infotype 2010 Wage Type Records

 

Figure 14 How the constituent wage types look in Payroll

 

 

These Infotype 2010 wage types are cumulated to a notional wage type in a PCR as shown below. Our requirement here was to aggregate all penalty/overtime payments and average them to form a rate over 6 weeks.

Figure 15 Aggregating the Seed Wage Type

The idea is to preferably place this custom PCR in the payroll schema immediately after the individual wage types get calculated, otherwise they may get written to RT or thrown away later.

 

 

I have placed this custom collating PCR just before X023 does an RT storage.

Figure 15.1 Subschema XAL9 or QAL9

 

The initial seed wage type and the final output wage type, in this case, 9901 and /199 need to be notional, i.e. not adding to net pay and not taxable.

 

The final /1XX wage type also needs to be available in the IT, otherwise AVERA will not pick it up. Hence, I have just created it with a dummy value in the following PCR.

Figure 16 Output wage type with a dummy value

This also means that after I have the final average rate in /199, I need to copy it to another wage type and throw /199 away in another PCR towards the end of my schema.

 

Going back to the payroll log and function AVERA, Rule Z018 takes the average value and places it into the rate field of /199. I have also inserted some more logic to get the number of payroll periods traversed into a separate variable.

Figure 17 Custom PCR writing to Output WT

The final average rate sits pretty in my output wage type in the IT as shown below.

The amount is still that dummy value added before.

Figure 18 Output WT with Average Rate

 

Conclusion

It is possible to meet averaging requirements entirely with payroll functional configuration. This saves the efforts of ABAP developers and helps focus their attention to more complex custom developments.

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll 
HCM Payroll

entry.title: Changing Field Color Based on Value - SuccessFactors Employee Central Custom MDF
entry.content:
In this blog, I want to share a simple yet impactful customization aimed at improving the visibility of asset status at a glance.

As part of a broader initiative to manage all company assets efficiently, we implemented a custom MDF (Metadata Framework) solution. One key requirement was to track whether an asset is currently active with a user. For this purpose, we created a picklist field with the values Yes and No to indicate the asset's status.

While functional, I wanted to take it a step further and add real business value by making the interface more intuitive. That’s when I came up with the idea of visually highlighting the status values — green for Active (Yes), and red for Inactive (No). This simple visual cue allows users to understand the status of an asset instantly, without needing to read each value, thereby improving usability and efficiency.

Let’s dive into the configuration details!

Step 1 : Begin by creating three fields:

  1. Is Asset Active? – A picklist field with values Yes and No to capture the asset’s current status.

  2. Asset Status - Active – A string field that will display the text Active when the asset is in use.

  3. Asset Status - Inactive – A string field that will display the text Inactive when the asset is not in use.

The reason for creating two separate string fields for displaying the status is to apply distinct conditional formatting for each scenario. This allows us to color-code the statuses individually — for example, applying a green highlight for the Active field and a red highlight for the Inactive field — which wouldn't be possible using a single field because currently single field doesnot support Dynamic Styling.

Step 2 : Create a UI Rule in Manage Configuration UI , which will set the value of the others two fields as active and inactive.Please find the Screenshots below of the rule and also the Stylings set for this field

 

The Font color of the Field 1 (Asset Status - Active) is set to custom1 and custom1 color is set to green so values in the field will be green in color in the UI.

The Font color of the Field 2 (Asset Status - Inactive) is set to Alert and Alert color is set to Red so values in the field will be Red in color in the UI.

How to set Color of the Fields?

Theme Manager - > Fine Tune -> Portlets 

The Following rule will set the Asset Status fields to active or inactive based on the Other field (Active/Inactive) and also control the visibility of the fields while creating records.However , we have a limitation currently where the vibisility of the fields cannot be controlled in the Portlet as mentioned in the below mentioned KBA

2540412 - UI Rule to Set visibility for field based on a separate field selection

Also , I tried setting the condition value which doesnt work as expected , when condition values are set the style is not respected and shows the field value in body text color when introspected the fields donot appear in the Manage Configuration UI when condition values are set.The Same has been confirmed by SAP Engineering Team that Manage Configuration UI is in maintenance mode and currently no new additions are added , so we cant expect this to be resolved anytime soon!

Now , Lets see the magic in action!

 

 

Here , The Solution works perfect except for the visibility of another Asset Status field which cannot be hidden due to the current limitations by the SAP (hope they come up with an action plan for it soon).

Conclusion :

In a nutshell, this is a very creative configuration which will add real value to the Business.This may look simple but holds so much value where records can be identified with a Glance!

Feel Free to Drop your thoughts / Comments , I am open for any Suggestions

entry.title: Showing Onboarding Documents in Employee Central Profile
entry.content:
Before You Start

For reflecting onboarding documents in new hire employee profile after job start date, on the first step you must configure standard creating custom data collection object procedure as defined in SAP Help Portal. You can see steps below link.

Link: Configuring Onboarding Data Collection with Default Data Collection Object Instance

Note: Please do not forget association between custom onboarding document collection and Onboarding data collection user configuration as you know userConfig and cust_subjectUser configuration. It ensures the relations among uploaded documents and new hire profile. Otherwise this configuration does not work in your system.

Creating A Custom MDF for EC Profile

After standard onboarding data collection configuration, you can start to create a custom MDF for EC profile.

• Set externalCode as User. In detail, visibility must be editable.
• MDF API visibility must be editable.

• Add association under of new object. Choose onbaording data collection MDF and set Type Valid When.

• Set visibility Editable. 
• Write cust_subjectUser to Destination Object Column
• Write externalCode to Source Object Column

After this process, you can configure an UI in Manage Configuration UI page.

After UI configuration, you must create a new portlet in Configure People Profile and add new custom MDF object in this section.

Set Up Integration Between Two Objects

• Go to Integration Centre page and create a new integration as below.

• Chose option as below.

• Choose custom onboarding document collection object as source.
• In Configuration Fields page, switch field mapping view at the right corner.
• Add custom EC profile object as destination object.

• Drag and drop subjectUser of source entity to externalCode of destination entity.
• Drag and drop externalCode of source entity to externalCode inside of association of destination entity association.
• Add modify since filter and set last run time in Filter and Sort page.

Schedule and Save your new integration.

Now you can see onboarding documents in new employees profile after they started to work. According to permissions, employees can access this portlet and download onboarding documents.

Click documents icon and see onboarding documents.

If you see more details in documents sheet, you can restrict to access about custom onboarding object in permission role as below.

Or you can change labels of your custom onboarding document object, such as cust_subjectUser field label can be User.

entry.title: Visual Enhancements in 1H 2025: What’s New in Performance & Goals and Succession Planning
entry.content:
Continuous Performance Management

  1. In the previous version of Continuous Performance-> Meeting, users were able to click anywhere on an activity card to navigate to the activity details page.

 

 In the current version of Continuous Performance -> Meeting, users need to click on the hyperlink within an activity card for navigation.

2. In the previous version of Continuous Performance page, the Activities, Achievements, and Meeting views retained a user's last View by selection.

In the current version of Continuous Performance page, the Activities, Achievements, and Meeting views do not retain a user's last View by selection. By default, the Activities and Meeting views display content from the View by Status tab, and the Achievements view shows the ‘All Achievements’ content.

3. In the previous version of Continuous Performance page, when there were no channels can be deleted, the ‘Select And Delete Channels’ button was still available on the channel panel.

 In the current version of Continuous Performance page, when there are no channels can be deleted, the Select and Delete Channels button is hidden.

4. In the previous version of Continuous Performance page, users could delete all channels within a category by selecting the checkbox next to the category header in the Delete Channels panel.

In the current version of Continuous Performance page, the Select All checkbox has been relocated to the position below a category header.

 

 

Succession & Development

  1. In the previous version, selecting Nomination History for a position opened a dialog that wasn't titled "Nomination History".

 

 

In the current version, the dialog is titled "Nomination History" in a new row added at the top.

 

 

2. In the previous version, in the filters panel of the position tile view, the Search, + Add, and Clear Filters buttons might not be visible on the screen unless users scrolled down to the bottom of the panel.

 

 

In the current version, the buttons are fixed at the bottom of the screen, so they are always visible. 

 

Go through these updates in the system for better understanding. Thanks for reading.

entry.title: How to restrict access to products to customers?
entry.content:
Hello all,

there is a requirement to restrict access to products to customer when creating sales orders in webstore.
Most of the sales orders will come through frontend store and the idea is that customer from certain type of industry for example construction will be only able to order construction specific products while products from other product groups/categories will remain hidden for this customer and customer will not be able order from these categories.

Where do you recommend to make the restriction, in frontend store based on e.g. product hierarchy nodes or in S/4 Public cloud using specific functionality?

Thank you for sharing your ideas.

entry.title: Connect with SAP Community at SAP Sapphire and Share Your Special SAP Event Moment!
entry.content:
Hello everyone! 

My name is Moshe Naveh, and I’m proud to be part of the SAP Community team. This year marks my 16th anniversary with this amazing Community, and one of the things I cherish most is meeting fellow members in person. Whether it’s at SAP TechEd, SAP Sapphire, or smaller community events, these gatherings always leave me inspired by the friendships, shared knowledge, and great energy we all bring to the table. 

With SAP Sapphire just around the corner, I can already feel the excitement building. Whether you’re attending in person or virtually, I know many of you are looking forward to learning something new, connecting with peers, and having some fun along the way. 

Meet SAP Community members speaking at SAP Sapphire  

This year, we want to amplify that excitement even before the event begins. Several SAP Community members are presenting at SAP Sapphire and have published blog posts about their upcoming sessions. These cover hot topics like Artificial Intelligence, SAP Business Suite, SAP Business Data Cloud, and more. 

Check out their posts to: 

- Get a sneak peek at their sessions 

- Join the conversation by answering a fun question 

- Share feedback and ideas 

 

Speaker’s blog post 

Speaker 

Fun question to answer in the blog post 

World champion analytics and performance in esports with SAP software 

Joschua Goetz 

(@Joschua_Goetz )

If you play DOTA2 or League of Legends, what do you think could be some of the most influential parts of the game we should look at? 

Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP that you can implement today 

Joanne Koh-Stewart

(@joanneks )

How are you currently driving digital transformation within your organization? 

 

Enhancing User Experience with SAP Fiori and AI  

Peter Spielvogel 

(@PeterSpielvogel)

How have you deployed SAP Fiori in your organization? 

Building the future of finance with SAP Taulia! 

Thomas Mehlkopf 

(@ThomasMehlkopf) 

As Finance or Procurement expert, what you are most looking forward to in SAP Sapphire. 

Helping secure BMS’s supply chain and drugs with track and trace solutions 

Aladdin Mandishah 

(@Aladdin)

When it comes to drug traceability, what's the one risk that keeps you up at night?  

An Architect’s guide: SAP Business Data Cloud and SAP Databricks 

Gaurish Dessai 

(@Gaurish-Dessai) 

As you prepare your data landscape for AI, which areas are your highest priorities? 

Responsible AI 

Sudhakar Singh 

(@sudhakarsingh)

Which factor of artificial intelligence do you think is the primary driver in user's trust in AI? 

How agentic AI revolutionizes the way SAP is running support

Michele Weynandt

(@MicheleWeynandt)

What are your thoughts/expectations/insights from SAP Sapphire 2025?

Powering process-centric, agentic AI strategies with SAP Signavio

Syeda Noor Zehra Naqvi 

( @NoorNaqvi )

How are you currently leveraging AI agents to increase your process efficiency? What are the current challenges you face when building your AI strategy?

Discover the Power of GenAI for Enterprise Transformation

Natalie Han

( @Nataliehan )

If an AI writes a joke and no one laughs, does it update its algorithm or just blame the humans? 😄

Future-proof your integration investment with AI

Arti Gopalan

(@artigopalan)

What specific capability or future direction of SAP Integration Suite   are you most eager to explore and would like to hear more about?

 Revolutionize SAP S/4HANA with AI and ISLM

Priti Dhingra

(@PritiDhingra )

Which specific topics related to ISLM interests you the most?

SAP and Databricks open a bold new era of data and AI

Kara Reed

( @KaraReed )

 Which use cases in your business could be accelerated or simplified by leveraging the zero-copy approach of SAP Databricks?

Also, as SAP Sapphire is all about learning and growth, I highly recommend exploring the Learning and Certification track sessions. These sessions cover a range of topics designed to help you when working in the SAP landscape. You'll get actionable insights that will help you with integrated tools, upskilling for AI-readiness, and enhance project success through certification and best practices. There is even a dedicated session about the SAP Community by @josh_bentley (Sr. Director Community Advocacy at SAP), @DanielleLarocca (GVP Strategic Aliiances at EPI-USE LIMITED and SAP Mentor) and by @anita_riegel  (VP & Head of SAP Industries & Experiences Key Stakeholder Management at SAP).

Come and say Hi😊!

Share a picture of your SAP event special moment 

I would like to end this post by starting a fun community activity:   

📷 Share a picture from an SAP event in the comments, along with a quick story about it. 

I’ll start by sharing this picture: 

Jitendra Kansal and Moshe Naveh at the SAP Community booth at SAP TechEd Bangalore 2013

 

 This photo was taken at SAP TechEd Bangalore 2013, during our celebration of 10 years of SAP Community. Standing next to me is my good friend and SAP colleague, @Jitendra Kumar Kansal. At the time, Jitendra was one of the leading contributors to the SAP Community’s Blog It Forward (BIF) challenge—a community initiative that brought so many of us closer through storytelling. 

Meeting Jitendra and other BIFers in person was a highlight of the event. We even had a special BIFers gathering, complete with some of my favorite snacks that I brought from home 😊.  

📸 What is your SAP Event special moment? Please share a picture and the story behind in the comments section below.  

Looking forward to connecting with you all during SAP Sapphire. Let’s make it a memorable event! 

📢 If you’re attending or speaking at SAP Sapphire and are interested in blogging about your experience, I’d love to support you! Feel free to reach out to me at community@sap.com and let’s work together to share your story with the SAP Community!

entry.title: SAP AI Security - How To: Tamperproof AI LLM's with SAP BTP Kyma and Enterprise Blockchain 🚀
entry.content:
This Blog is hand written...

Continuing the blog series on SAP and Enterprise Blockchain, which started with the, Why I love SAP and Blockchain Databases and why you should too 🚀 and went through the journey of SAP Enterprise Architecture: Positioning Blockchain Database as an Enterprise Technology Standard 🚀 and SAP Enterprise Architecture: Let the Use Case find the Blockchain🚀 and the Enterprise Blockchain Business Capability Map Enterprise Architecture: Enterprise Blockchain Platform Business Capability Map 🚀 and subsequently discussed all manner of SAP related Enterprise Blockchain use cases which can be implemented today with the SAP BTP Kyma Service and Enterprise Blockchain software (eg atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP), and which included a how to guide on how to run your own Enterprise Blockchain on the SAP BTP Kyma Running Your Own Blockchain on The SAP BTP Kyma Trial: A Hands On How To Guide 🚀 then, this blog is going to explain a very simple way to secure SAP AI and AI LLM's with Enterprise Blockchain running on the SAP BTP Kyma Service.

In my opinion, Enterprise Blockchain is the world's most tamper resistant store of data, providing tamper resistance to a level which cannot be achieved by traditional Enterprise Database products and technology. I've explained this in detail in this blog: Why I love SAP and Blockchain Databases and why you should too 🚀 

 

A lot has been written, and is being written on SAP and AI and LLM security, and rightly so, because at the same time this technology is both a huge opportunity and a huge risk. The opportunities are vast, in my opinion SAP & AI basically speeds up decision making across any dimension where it is applied, and therefore the corresponding risk is, can we trust the advice and recommendation that the SAP AI is giving us, what if the AI has been tampered with by a bad actor ?

There are some very nice articles and documents on this subject, here are a few:

. Assessing the Security of Large Language Models - Exploring vulnerabilities, threats, and potential malicious use cases for generative AI as presented by the rapid proliferation of LLMs

. Defending Against AI Security Risks by Turning LLM... - SAP Community thanks to @JayThvV 

. Navigating the Generative AI Era: Blockchain is ke... - SAP Community thanks to @mirksen 

. Secure your LLM: Consuming SAP Generative AI deplo... - SAP Community thanks to @AronMac who drew the perfect picture of the problem:

This is Aron's picture (may I please ask for retrospective permission to reproduce it here) and it perfectly identifies where the risk is, read Aron's blog for more details

.  Secure Large Language Model Access 

 

The problem is how can we make AI LLM's tamper proof and therefore verifiably trustable ?

tldr:

The technical solution is basically a variation of this one,Cyber Security Protection for S/4HANA Backups with Enterprise Blockchain and SAP BTP Kyma 🚀, we simply run a background which takes a digital finger print of the LLM at regular intervals and compares the hash each time to the original digital fingerprint hash which we have stored in the Enterprise Blockchain.

The What:

An AI LLM is Data, there is a process to get from zero to operational, and that is, 1) Initial Data Intake, 2) Data Conditioning, 3) Model Development, 4) Specialisation Phase, 5) Production Integration, 6) LifeCycle Oversight and each phase of the process can be secured and protected with Enterprise Blockchain running on the SAP BTP Kyma.

Initial Data Intake
Inputs: Collected content such as user-generated inputs, external datasets, sensor readings, or media files.
Risks: Unauthorized data sourcing, risk of ingesting biased or harmful content, and accidental inclusion of proprietary materials.
Safeguard: Store a cryptographic hash of each backup in the Enterprise Blockchain (SAP BTP Kyma). Automate batch jobs to periodically re-hash the current data and verify it against the original to ensure integrity and traceability.

Data Conditioning
Inputs: Raw collected information that requires transformation into usable formats through parsing, validation, and structuring.
Risks: Accidental disclosure of sensitive patterns, inconsistent formatting leading to faulty models, and compliance oversights.
Safeguard: Generate a unique digital signature of the conditioned dataset and record it in the Enterprise Blockchain via SAP BTP Kyma. Schedule automated processes to regularly verify new signatures against the stored baseline for authenticity checks.

Model Development
Inputs: Curated datasets that serve as the learning foundation for model algorithms.
Risks: Loss of dataset lineage, misuse of confidential training sources, and exposure to adversarial inputs.
Safeguard: Digitally fingerprint the dataset versions and register them on the SAP BTP Kyma-based Blockchain. Use recurring batch processes to ensure the data used for training remains tamper-free and aligned with the original set.

Specialization Phase
Inputs: Base models enhanced using narrowly focused or domain-specific datasets for accuracy improvements.
Risks: Fine-tuning errors due to data drift, accidental overwrites of optimized parameters, or non-compliant tuning sources.
Safeguard: Secure a hash of the updated models and input data in the SAP BTP Kyma Blockchain. Implement scheduled revalidation by hashing updated files and comparing them to the secured originals to prevent silent regressions.

Production Integration
Inputs: Trained models deployed to serve real-time inputs, APIs, or embedded environments.
Risks: Exposure of inference logic, tampering with deployed assets, or leakage of sensitive model behavior.
Safeguard: Record a hashed snapshot of all deployment artifacts within the SAP BTP Kyma Blockchain. Automate monitoring routines that continuously compare current deployment fingerprints to the authenticated versions.

Lifecycle Oversight
Inputs: System logs, telemetry data, and user interaction feedback used to update models or flag issues.
Risks: Silent model degradation, undocumented model mutations, or targeted manipulation attempts such as prompt injections.
Safeguard: Store a verifiable hash of lifecycle data and changes into the Enterprise Blockchain on SAP BTP Kyma. Set up recurring batch tasks to re-hash current states and detect unauthorized alterations or drifts in model behavior.

The How:

It all sounds easy doesn't it, and that's because it really is, all you need is:

. Your AI LLM's 

. A regular Backup of the AI LLM's - this should be automated

. Take a digital fingerprint hash of each backup of the AI LLM's (I wrote about that here: Cyber Security Protection for S/4HANA Backups with Enterprise Blockchain and SAP BTP Kyma 🚀 - this should be automate

. Get the SAP BTP and the BTP Kyma Service

. Deploy Enterprise Blockchain on the SAP BTP Kyma Service (here's a how to guide: Running Your Own Blockchain on The SAP BTP Kyma Trial: A Hands On How To Guide 🚀

. Store the first digital fingerprint of each LLM backup in the Enterprise Blockchain running on the SAP BTP Kyma - this should be automated

. Run a regular job to take a digital fingerprint of the AI LLM and compare the hash to the hash stored in the Enterprise Blockchain running on the SAP BTP Kyma - this should be automated

The technical architecture would look something like this:

 

Wrapping up, conclusions:

. Trustable Enterprise AI depends on Data being stored in the Enterprise Blockchain

Enterprise Blockchain is:

. The Digital Transformation of Information Security 

. The Next Generation Data Integrity, Originality, Confidentiality Protection

. Re-imagining Information Security

. Natively, out of the box, due to its special characteristics the strongest, hardest, most resilient Enterprise Database product 

Enterprise Blockchain is both:

. a Secure Store

. a Secure Communication Channel

The configurable Enterprise Blockchain Wallet enables you to store Big Data 'Off-Chain' and the hashes of the Big Data are stored safely and securely on the Enterprise Blockchain Database.

The good news is, as we discussed in the previous blog, this is no longer hype, we can do all of this today, and now, within the SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem there are enabling technology Blockchain Products designed and built by SAP Experts specifically for the needs of SAP Customers to make doing Blockchain and SAP easy, and so you can do SAP and Blockchain, today it's real and there's nothing stopping you.

So what are we waiting for ? Oh yeah, more use cases, ok, that will be the next blog. 

What do you think, are the words AI, Blockchain, Web3, Distributed Ledger Technology, starting to appear in your Company's visions and technology visions ? What use cases are you looking at ? Let's chat about it in the comments.

For now, over and out.

Andy Silvey.

Independent SAP Technical Architect (you might also find my SAP S/4HANA RISE & BTP Toolbox interesting:  🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰  and CEO of atkrypto (.) io

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 26 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 11 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto inc, an startup whose ambition is to make Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto (.) io's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto (.) io is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.

entry.title: Scoping the integration to EC of existing Compensation and Variable Pay templates
entry.content:
Introduction

Please find below the questions I asked a few days ago to a customer in a one hour call to understand the complexity and as a consequence the level of effort of integrating 3 non-EC Compensation templates and 2 non-EC Variable Pay templates to Employee Central which went live for them recently.

 

 

Variable Pay templates

 

  • Earnings
    • Are target bonus amounts based on actual earnings or contractual salary data ?
      • If actual earnings:
        • Does the payroll system have the data on Target Bonus % changes ?
        • Is there a report already built in Payroll/ABAP that can provide the actual numbers ?
        • Is there a will from leadership to move away from actual earnings and use contractual salary ?
      • If contractual earning: are both the Annualized Salary data and Target Bonus % data currently loaded in EC and with effective dates covering the entire past fiscal year ?

 

 

Compensation templates

 

  • Data consistency 
    • Are there Compensation templates where data gets loaded as of different effective dates (for example Salary used for merit % calculation is as of 12/31/2025 but Job Title is shown as of 2/1/2025) ?
    • Do you use Global Assignment or Concurrent Employment features ?
    • Are Pay Ranges loaded in "Manage Pay Job and Org structure" ?
    • Are they used to trigger Compa Ratio calculation in Comp Info ? (meaning have they been tested on top of being loaded)
    • Is Compa Ratio or Range Penetration used in some of the existing Compensation templates ?

 

 

All templates

 

  • Eligibility rules across templates
    • Is there data used in eligibility rules that currently lives outside of Employee Central ? (excluding UserID exceptions). 
    • Is there a need to use data from the Time Off module (if in use) to exclude by userID in Compensation or prorate records in Variable Pay templates ?
  • Publish back to EC
    • Is there one or more of template for which we will need to build complex publish back logic, for example different event dates or even reasons for different countries ?
    • Do we want different event reasons in case of just merit vs merit + lumpsum etc.
  • Statements
    • Assumption for this integration work is that statements will not be edited as they get their data from the worksheets that will be integrated to EC. Please confirm.

 

 

 

 

 

All the best,

Xavier

 

(If you found this blog useful please consider giving it a Like)

We’re looking for speakers for SAP Inside Track Belgium, taking place on Saturday, September 20th, 2025, at Amista’s new office in Mechelen!

Whether you are a developer, consultant, architect, business user, or SAP enthusiast — if you have a story, project, lesson learned, or hands-on demo you’d like to share, we’d love to hear from you.

entry.title: Regression testing recommendations for SAP Screen Personas flavors in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
I recently received an email from a colleague on behalf of a customer they are helping in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud migration project.

How much regression testing do you recommend to customers, both in the case of an SAP S/4HANA upgrade and the implementation of service packs for SAP Screen Personas? Since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is frequently upgraded, do customers regularly conduct regression testing for all of the adapted screens?

In this post, I’ll discuss our recommendations for regression testing of SAP Screen Personas flavors in the context of SAP S/4HANA upgrades.

TLDR; Test everything before going live with any changes. Leave enough time to do this.

Regression testing ensures that software works after upgrades

According to GitHub, “Regression testing is a process that ensures that software still performs as expected, even after a change.” In the context of the question above, the changes we are talking about include upgrades to SAP S/4HANA or new SAP Screen Personas service packs. Both represent changes to the IT environment.

When I worked on IT infrastructure management tools in a previous company, the first question we asked customers when they reported that something was broken was “what did you change?” Most problems we encountered were self-inflicted, the result of changes to something in their production environment. The key to catching these issues before they cause an impact on the business is thorough testing of any changes to software, hardware, configurations, or anything.

We recommend regression testing on SAP Screen Personas flavors

If you are performing any upgrade to your business-critical ERP system, we recommend regression testing for *everything* that changes. Ideally, your development or quality assurance (QA) system mirrors your production system, so you can perform the update on your dev system, test that everything works, then implement the same upgrade on your QA and production systems. By verifying that all the individual systems work in a low-risk setting (dev and QA), you will avoid any surprises in the production system.

Regression testing takes time. Do not wait until the last minute. You want to make sure you have enough time before you go live to fix any issues that you find.

Playwright is a common tool for testing web apps, including SAP Screen Personas. You can run a series of tests to ensure that what worked before also works after your upgrade. One key to success here is maintaining the tests that you create. Testing can be manual and/or automated.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud upgrades don’t break SAP Screen Personas flavors

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we make upgrades approximately every six months (2408, 2502, 2508, etc.). Generally, this does not cause any problems for SAP Screen Personas flavors. The exception would be if a control ID in the transaction changed and a script or other change in the system pointed to that now obsolete object. This is not common.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we release feature packs (FPS0, FPS1, etc.) approximately every six months. Customers can consume them at their own pace, allowing them time to test any parts that have changed since their last update. In this case too, we do not expect any issues with SAP Screen Personas flavors. The only time something would break in a flavor is if a control ID in the transaction changed and a script or other change in the flavor pointed to that now obsolete object. Here too, this is not common.

We engineer SAP Screen Personas upgrades to be non-disruptive

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the SAP Screen Personas functionality is built in as Adapt UI for Classic Apps. You access it through the same user menu as Adapt UI (for SAP Fiori apps built with SAPUI5). Since the user does not need to update anything on their own, we test the Adapt UI capabilities before we include them in the larger ERP upgrade. We are part of the “release train” for all the semi-annual releases.

Since we don’t know how you have built your flavors, we strongly recommend that you test your SAP Screen Personas flavors to ensure that everything works with each system upgrade. There are no SAP Screen Personas service packs with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Any new functionality simply appears in your system. This is the beauty of the cloud.

I cannot remember a time when we introduced new functionality that broke any flavors that customers built. There have been many occasions where we introduced easier ways to do things (adaptive flavors, viewports, scripting). In some cases, this might warrant rebuilding flavors to improve performance or streamline your maintenance. Of course, if you choose to keep things as they are, they will still work.

For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, we release SAP Screen Personas service packs around once per year. You can choose to install them or not. In our release announcement and documentation, we clearly explain what’s new and why it matters to you. If you choose to install the latest SAP Screen Personas service pack, we encourage you to perform regression testing on the flavors that you use in production.

Our own service pack upgrades typically do not cause any problems for SAP Screen Personas flavors. We aim not to introduce changes into the product that break existing functionality. It is possible that you might have implemented something in a script or applet that uses an older version of SAPUI5 that we moved up to the current version. It would be very rare that this would disrupt your script, as the SAPUI5 team takes extreme care not to deprecate functionality.

We usually release new SAP Screen Personas innovations every year

If you want to plan your upgrades and testing cycles, here is a graphic that shows our release schedule and support commitment for SAP Screen Personas.

Moving flavors from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is possible

As many customers are moving forward on their RISE with SAP journeys, we are starting to hear questions about migrating flavors from an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition environment to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. For some organizations, their ultimate goal is to run everything on public cloud; private cloud is a temporary transition.

There is no direct path to transport flavors from a private cloud system to a public cloud system. This is obvious, since they are completely disconnected environments. It is possible to use the export – import flavor capability to move your flavors to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, then adjust them if necessary.

Some features in private cloud, such as theming, RFC support, or Slipstream Engine-specific functionality, are not supported in public cloud, so please plan accordingly.

If you are moving your flavors from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there are likely many other IT changes that are happening at the same time. We recommend you take this opportunity to review your flavors, update the documentation so any new team members can maintain them, and only move those that are actively used.

Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Ai Pre-created Segments
entry.content:
Hello!

In order to use the AI segments, first an account owner needs to review the document with the SAP Emarsys AI Terms, which is required to unlock the AI features: this can be done at Management > Consent Management menu

1 - Consent Management - SAP Emarsys AI Terms | SAP Help Portal

After that, the prerequisites are described at the document below, but mainly:

Emarsys collects data from various sources such as channel engagement, web behavior tracking information, and uploaded order data to your account. The following data tables are populated and required to run AI Segmentation:

  • Email sends
  • Email clicks
  • Email opens
  • Web Extend sessions
  • Smart Insight sales
  • Smart Insight contacts

As additional criteria, you need to have:

  • All of the above tables simultaneously (if any of them is missing, some or all AI segment calculations will fail).
  • At least 7 months of respective data collected.
  • The last 6 months of backtests should be successful.
  • Model performance must be above a certain threshold.

2 - Prerequisites for AI features | SAP Help Portal

Then, you will finally have the AI segments unlocked and you can read more about how they work in general and how each of them work at the resources below:

3 - AI segments | SAP Help Portal

4 - Available AI Segment Templates and Options | SAP Help Portal

 

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Re: search restriction with the following parameters:
entry.content:
What does 1 = 0 mean? And what would change if it would be User: customergroup? Would the result be the same?

EDIT:

I found the answer. So in case you will access product detail page with restriction:
Type: Product
User: anonymous
Restriction: 1=0

it will probably return 404 or product not found. 

Restriction describes that restriction works for Product type and all subtypes (including VariantProduct).
User field describes that this applies for anonymous customers.
Restriction field describes that it will always be false.

For example, if you are not logged in customer, accessing variant product on product detail page, you will see 404 or page not found, since in backed flexible search query will return empty result for that search.

entry.title: Re: remove last character in a string
entry.content:
Sometimes the symbol looks like a ' # ' is not actually a hash symbol. Instead, it can be a carriage return (CR) or newline character, especially when you're using:

CALL METHOD cl_gui_textedit=>get_textstream

or processing multiline text from GUI controls (CL_GUI_TEXTEDIT, SPLIT using CL_ABAP_CHAR_UTILITIES=>NEWLINE etc.).

In such cases, even though the last character appears to be #, it's a non-printable character.

Solution (with hex check)

Here’s a reliable way to detect the actual last character and remove it only if needed (e.g. it's a # or control character):

DATA: lv_value    TYPE string VALUE 'ABCDE#',  " Input string (may have unwanted char at end)
      lv_len      TYPE i,                      " Length of the string
      lv_lastval  TYPE c LENGTH 1,             " Last character of the string
      lv_hex      TYPE string,                 " Hex value of the last character
      lv_newval   TYPE string.                 " Final cleaned value

" Step 1: Find the length of the string
lv_len = strlen( lv_value ).
SUBTRACT 1 FROM lv_len.  " Adjust to point to last character (0-based index)

" Step 2: Get the last character
lv_lastval = lv_value+lv_len(1).

" Step 3: Convert last character to hex using built-in function module
CALL FUNCTION 'NLS_STRING_CONVERT_FROM_SYS'
  EXPORTING
    lang_used = sy-langu       " Use system language
    source    = lv_lastval     " Character to convert
    to_fe     = 'MS '          " Target frontend encoding (Microsoft)
  IMPORTING
    result    = lv_hex         " Hex value of the character
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS    = 1.             " Simple error catch

" Step 4: Check if the character is '#' (hex 23)
IF lv_hex = '23'.  " ASCII hex for '#'
  lv_newval = lv_value+0(lv_len).  " Remove last character
ELSE.
  lv_newval = lv_value.            " Keep original if not '#'
ENDIF.
  • NLS_STRING_CONVERT_FROM_SYS helps get the true hex value of a character.
  • This avoids misleading visual characters (like #) that are actually CR, LF, etc.

I hope this helps! Let me know your thoughts or if you have any questions.
Thanks,

Akshay Anil 
Technical Consultant, SAP ABAP | BTP | Fiori | Ui5

entry.title: Re: Help with NoClassDefFoundError for com/sap/aii/securi...
entry.content:
In a JavaMapping in SAP PI when you want to access KeyStoreManager you do it using Class SAPSecurityResources in com.sap.aii.af.svc.facade API found in PO by default. 
 
If you want to access java keystore in J2EE applications you cannot use SAPSecurityResources for some reason and it keeps generating the NoClassDefFoundError Exception. You can access keystore manager using JNDI lookup using javax.naming.Context (Code below). However it doesn't return the KeyStoreManager interface found in SAPSecurityResources and thus you cannot use com.sap.aii.af.lib.facade API to get a reference to KeyStoreManager.
 
When you want to access KeyStoreManager from javax.naming.Context object using lookup (as below) it returns com.sap.engine.services.keystore.interfaces.KeystoreManagerWrapper interface.

Example Code
Hashtable <String, String> env = new Hashtable<>();

// Replace with your actual configuration
env.put(Context.INITIAL_CONTEXT_FACTORY, "com.sap.engine.services.jndi.InitialContextFactoryImpl");
env.put(Context.PROVIDER_URL, "hostname:50004"); // P4 port for JNDI lookup
env.put(Context.SECURITY_PRINCIPAL, "username"); // Replace with your actual username
env.put(Context.SECURITY_CREDENTIALS, "password"); // Replace with your actual password
env.put(Context.URL_PKG_PREFIXES, "com.sap.engine.services");

try {
    Context jndiContext = new InitialContext(env);
    KeystoreManagerWrapper ksManager = (KeystoreManagerWrapper) jndiContext.lookup("keystore");
    // Use the ksManager as needed
} catch (NamingException e) {
    e.printStackTrace();
}​

You can then use the object as needed, for example
// Retrieve and log aliases
List<String> aliases = ksManager.aliases("alias");
System.out.println("Aliases found: " + aliases.size());
for (String alias : aliases) {
    System.out.println("Alias: " + alias);
}

Explanation:
When you feed in your env variable to InitialContext it does return an object, so with a little help from the internet I analyzed the object:
import javax.naming.Context;
import javax.naming.InitialContext;
import javax.naming.NamingException;
import java.lang.reflect.Method;
import java.util.Hashtable;

public class JNDIExample {
  public static void main(String[] args) {
    Hashtable < String, String > env = new Hashtable < > ();

    // Replace with your actual configuration
    env.put(Context.INITIAL_CONTEXT_FACTORY, "com.sap.engine.services.jndi.InitialContextFactoryImpl");
    env.put(Context.PROVIDER_URL, "hostname:50004"); // P4 port for JNDI lookup
    env.put(Context.SECURITY_PRINCIPAL, "username"); // Replace with your actual username
    env.put(Context.SECURITY_CREDENTIALS, "password"); // Replace with your actual password
    env.put(Context.URL_PKG_PREFIXES, "com.sap.engine.services");

    try {
      Context jndiContext = new InitialContext(env);
      Object obj = jndiContext.lookup("keystore"); // Replace with your actual JNDI name

      // Check if lookup returned a null object
      if (obj == null) {
        System.out.println("Lookup returned null");
        return;
      }

      // Print the class of the returned object
      Class < ? > objClass = obj.getClass();
      System.out.println("Class of the returned object: " + objClass.getName());

      // Print the interfaces implemented by the returned object
      System.out.println("Interfaces implemented by the returned object:");
      Class < ? > [] interfaces = objClass.getInterfaces();
      for (Class < ? > iface : interfaces) {
        System.out.println(" - " + iface.getName());
      }

      // Print the methods available in the returned object's class
      System.out.println("Methods available in the returned object's class:");
      Method[] methods = objClass.getMethods();
      for (Method method: methods) {
        System.out.println(" - " + method.getName());
      }

    } catch (NamingException e) {
      e.printStackTrace();
    }
  }
}

The code above prints the following log:

/*
Interfaces implemented by the returned object:
- com.sap.engine.services.keystore.interfaces.KeystoreManagerWrapper
- com.sap.engine.services.rmi_p4.RemoteRef
- java.rmi.Remote
Methods available in the returned object's class:
- equals
- destroyKeystoreView
- renameEntry
- getObjectInfo
- checkPermission
- toString
- getSecurityConnector
- getKeystore
- getKeystoreViewAliases
- aliases
- findAlias
- existKeystoreView
- createKeystoreView
- hashCode
- size
- readEntry
- setProperty
- getProperty
- getKeystoreViewProperties
- writeEntry
- exists
- removeProperty
- deleteEntry
- isKeyEntry
- getProxyClass
- newProxyInstance
- isProxyClass
- getInvocationHandler
- getClass
- notify
- notifyAll
- wait
- wait
- wait
 */

The location of the API containing the interface KeystoreManagerWrapper :  

/*
/usr/sap/<SID>/J<instance_number>/j2ee/cluster/bin/interfaces/keystore_api/lib/private/sap.com~tc~je~keystore_api~API.jar
*/
update with your <SID> and J<instance_number>

Download the API File using plain java or just ask basis team at this point ;).
Example Code
import java.io.File;
import java.io.FileInputStream;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.io.OutputStream;
import javax.servlet.ServletException;
import javax.servlet.annotation.WebServlet;
import javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet;
import javax.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest;
import javax.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse;

@WebServlet("/downloadFile")
public class FileDownloadServlet extends HttpServlet {
    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;

    protected void doGet(HttpServletRequest request, HttpServletResponse response) throws ServletException, IOException {
        // Full path to the file on the server
        String filePath = "/usr/sap/<SID>/J<instance_number>/j2ee/cluster/bin/interfaces/keystore_api/lib/private/sap.com~tc~je~keystore_api~API.jar";
        File file = new File(filePath);

        // Check if the file exists and is not a directory
        if (file.exists() && file.isFile()) {
            // Set the content type based on the file type
            response.setContentType("application/java-archive");
            response.setHeader("Content-Disposition", "attachment;filename=" + file.getName());

            // Read the file and write it to the response output stream
            try (FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream(file); OutputStream os = response.getOutputStream()) {

                byte[] buffer = new byte[1024];
                int bytesRead;
                while ((bytesRead = fis.read(buffer)) != -1) {
                    os.write(buffer, 0, bytesRead);
                }
            }
        } else {
            response.getWriter().println("File not found: " + filePath);
        }
    }
}​​

entry.title: Best approach to define corporate colors in crystal reports 2020?
entry.content:
SAP Crystal Reports I'm working on migrating our 2016 crystal reports to 2020.

My company has a corporate color palette (13+ colors) and requirements as far as the ordering of these colors in our reports. For example, first series should always be Color X, second series should always be Color Y, and so on. This is all implemented in the existing reports. 

The catch: I'm having trouble identifying how the corporate colors were embedded in the 2016 reports. Could it just have been done manually? I'm searching in Crystal Desktop with a report open and there is zero indication. We use CRChart but I don't see any color-related macros in use. 

I hoped to find some centralized place the colors were defined...but haven't found it. 

It's not a blocker, but it would be good to have a centralized solution identified in case colors change, ordering changes, etc. 

Appreciate any help.
